qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: The purpose of instruction location counter in an assembler is _______

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Mention the pass number for each of the following activities that occur in a two pass assembler: A. object code generation B. literals added to literal table C. listing printed D. address resolution of local symbols

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Write short answers to the following: i. Which of the following macros can put a macro assembler into an infinite loop? .MACRO M1,X .MACRO M2,X .IF EQ,X .IF EQ,X M1 X+1 M2 X .ENDC .ENDC .IF NE,X .IF NE,X .WORD X .WORD X+1 .ENDC .ENDC .ENDM .ENDM Give an example call that does so.

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: A simple two-pass assembler does the following in the first pass: A. It allocates space for the literals. B. It computes the total length of the program. C. It builds the symbol table for the symbols and their values. D. It generates code for all the load and store register instructions. E. None of
